text.title: Multimodal VUI Design Anti-Patterns
text.summary: State-sync failures, platform template incompatibilities, timeout edge cases, and agent-specific gotchas in multimodal VUI design.

antipattern.description: AI-generated flow spec misses state sync when user speaks while simultaneously touching.
antipattern.reason: Multimodal state sync on simultaneous input is an edge case agent-generated specs routinely miss. Add it as an explicit review checklist item in every multimodal spec review.

antipattern.description: Using Echo Show APL templates on Google Nest Hub without adaptation.
antipattern.reason: APL is Amazon-specific; Google uses Action Builder with Rich Responses and Canvas. Platform-specific templates require separate implementations. Simulator rendering also differs from actual hardware.

antipattern.description: Agent-generated APL JSON using deprecated APL version features.
antipattern.reason: APL evolves between versions. Without specifying the exact target APL version in the prompt, agents default to older patterns that fail on current firmware.

antipattern.description: Confusing "fallback" (user cannot use primary input) with "error" (input understood but failed).
antipattern.reason: Fallback paths handle inability to use a modality; error paths handle understood-but-failed interactions. Conflating them produces flows where errors are handled with the wrong recovery mechanism.

antipattern.description: Wake-word false positives disrupting visual UI experience.
antipattern.reason: AI cannot solve wake-word false positive rate at the UX layer — this is a hardware problem. Design visual UI to be resilient to unexpected voice activation, with visible state indicators when VUI is active.

antipattern.description: VUI timeout set too short for complex visual content without accessibility consideration.
antipattern.reason: Default timeouts from agent-generated specs are typically optimized for average users. Elderly users and users with cognitive disabilities need at minimum 10s for simple responses, 60s for multi-step forms.
